name: Legal Files Case Management Software API
what_it_does: Legal Files case management software REST API for insurance defense, corporate legal departments, government agencies, and law firms managing litigation, claims, and legal matter tracking. Enables AI agents to manage matter and file creation for corporate legal and government case management automation, handle party and contact management for litigation roster automation, access document management and correspondence tracking for legal file management automation, retrieve task and deadline management for legal docket automation, manage litigation hold and preservation tracking for e-discovery compliance automation, handle claims and insurance reserve tracking for insurance defense matter management, access budget and matter financial management for corporate legal spend management automation, retrieve reporting and analytics for legal department KPI tracking, manage relationship tracking between matters and parties for complex litigation automation, and integrate Legal Files with document management systems (iManage, NetDocuments), ERP, and litigation support platforms for corporate legal operations.
use_cases: Managing insurance defense matters from claims agents
Tracking corporate legal matters from GC agents
Managing government legal files from agency agents
Controlling legal spend from e-billing agents
not_for: Plaintiff personal injury (use SmartAdvocate or CasePeer)
Small law firm practice management (use Clio or MyCase)
best_when: An insurance company, self-insured corporation, government legal department, or defense firm wanting AI agents to automate matter management, document tracking, litigation hold, claims management, and legal spend reporting within Legal Files.
avoid_when: LITIGATION HOLD EVIDENCE PRESERVATION ACCURACY: Automated litigation hold management in Legal Files must correctly identify all custodians and data sources relevant to the matter; automated hold with incomplete custodian scope creates evidence spoliation risk with potential sanctions; implement litigation hold scope validation protocol for automated holds. ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE DOCUMENT CLASSIFICATION: Automated document intake and management in Legal Files must classify privileged documents (attorney-client communication, attorney work product) before any external disclosure; automated document production without privilege review creates inadvertent privilege waiver and confidentiality breach. INSURANCE RESERVE ADEQUACY FOR CLAIMS: Automated insurance reserve management in Legal Files must be set and adjusted by qualified insurance claims professional; automated reserve setting based on algorithmic calculation without adjuster review creates reserve inaccuracy and potential bad faith exposure for under-reserving high-value claims.

auth.notes: Legal Files uses API key and OAuth authentication. REST API with JSON. Springfield, Illinois HQ. Founded 1992. Private. Insurance defense, corporate legal, and government agency focus. 1,000+ installations. On-premise and cloud deployment. Insurance claims management integration. iManage and NetDocuments integration. Competes with Aderant, LexisNexis CaseMap, and Litify for insurance defense and corporate legal case management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual subscription with user and module licensing
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual per-user subscription; core matter management, claims, and document modules; on-premise and cloud options; implementation professional services
pricing.notes: Springfield IL. Private. Founded 1992. Insurance defense and government legal focus. 1,000+ installations. Annual subscription with implementation.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; matter and document queries throttled; no webhooks — polling required

known_agent_gotchas: NO WEBHOOKS — ALL MATTER AND CLAIMS EVENTS REQUIRE POLLING: Legal Files does not support native webhooks; automated matter management, claims reserve updates, and document events require scheduled polling; design polling aligned with insurance defense and corporate legal daily workflow cycles

LITIGATION HOLD CUSTODIAN SCOPE COMPLETENESS: Automated litigation hold creation in Legal Files must include all known custodians and data systems at matter inception; automated hold with insufficient custodian scope creates evidence preservation gap; implement litigation hold scope questionnaire before automated hold initiation for any matter with litigation potential

INSURANCE RESERVE BAD FAITH MONITORING: Automated insurance reserve management must maintain reserve adequacy documentation; automated reserve reduction without documented claim development justification creates bad faith exposure for insurance carrier; claims reserve changes require adjuster authorization and file notation

On-premise Legal Files installation API network access — Legal Files on-premise installations require automation infrastructure with direct network access to on-premise server; cloud-hosted automation cannot reach on-premise Legal Files without VPN or secure tunnel; verify deployment model before architecture design

Government agency FOIA and public records exception for matter data — government legal department Legal Files implementations may contain matter data subject to FOIA disclosure exemptions (attorney-client privilege, attorney work product, law enforcement sensitive); automated matter data exports must apply FOIA exemption classification before any disclosure

Claims expense code and billing guideline compliance — automated outside counsel billing entry in Legal Files for insurance defense matters must comply with carrier billing guidelines (task-based billing codes, AFA arrangements, billing rate schedules); automated billing without guideline compliance creates invoice rejection and outside counsel relationship friction
agent_readiness.security_notes: Legal case management. SOC2. API key/OAuth. US. Privileged legal documents, claims, and confidential matter data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Legal case management. SOC2. API key/OAuth. US. Privileged matter, claims, and confidential legal data.
